title: Forces & Newton's Laws
subject: physics
summary: This topic applies Newton's laws to analyze motion under forces.
SPTG heavily tests free body diagrams, friction, and multi-object systems.
Success depends on setting correct equations, not complex math.
coreConcepts: Newton's 1st law: object stays at rest or constant velocity unless net force acts.
Newton's 3rd law: action-reaction pairs are equal and opposite.
Normal force is perpendicular to surface, not always equal to weight.
Tension is same throughout a massless rope.
Always analyze forces per object using FBD.
derive: On incline: weight splits into mg sinθ (down slope) and mg cosθ (normal).
For multiple objects: treat system together or separately.
If system moves together, internal forces cancel.
formulas: net force = sum of all forces
traps: Normal force is not always equal to mg.
Friction is μN only when at maximum or moving.
Do not mix action-reaction pairs within same body.
In incline problems, resolve forces correctly.
For connected systems, do not double count forces.
Sign convention mistakes lead to wrong answers.

sptgSet.question: A 2 kg object accelerates at 3 m/s^2. Find net force.

Block on incline (30°), mass 2 kg. Find component down slope (g=10).
Two masses 2 kg and 3 kg connected, pulled with 25 N. Find acceleration.
Tension in system above (2 kg block).
A 5 kg object on incline with μ=0.2. Find friction.

A 3 kg object on rough surface, μ=0.3. Find minimum force to move.
Block pulled with constant velocity. What is net force?
